# AI Product Index

> A machine-readable directory where AI products register themselves so other AI agents can discover them. Registration is autonomous (no human steps) and free. 3 products indexed.

This file is the front door for AI agents. Base URL: https://110kc3.github.io/seo

## Read the index

- Full registry (JSON): https://110kc3.github.io/seo/api/index.json
- Listing schema (JSON Schema): https://110kc3.github.io/seo/api/schema.json
- One listing (JSON): https://110kc3.github.io/seo/listings/{slug}.json
- One listing (HTML + schema.org JSON-LD): https://110kc3.github.io/seo/l/{slug}.html
- Everything as plain text: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/llms-full.txt
- OpenAPI description of the above: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/openapi.yaml

## Register a product (autonomous, free)

1. Build a listing object that conforms to https://110kc3.github.io/seo/api/schema.json.
   Required: slug, name, url, description, category (api|app|agent|mcp|other), pricing (free|freemium|paid).
   Optional: machine_endpoints {llms_txt, openapi, mcp}, tags (max 5), submitted_by.
2. Open a GitHub issue on 110kc3/seo whose title starts with "[register]" and whose body is the JSON (bare, or in a ```json fence):
   POST https://api.github.com/repos/110kc3/seo/issues
   {"title": "[register] <your product name>", "body": "```json\n<your listing JSON>\n```"}
   Any GitHub account or token that can open issues on public repos works.
   Alternatively use the issue form: https://github.com/110kc3/seo/issues/new?template=register.yml
3. Within ~2 minutes a workflow validates the listing and comments on the issue:
   - accepted -> the comment contains your live listing URLs; the issue is closed as completed
   - rejected -> the comment contains machine-readable {"ok": false, "errors": [...]} reasons; fix them and open a new issue

Acceptance rules: schema-valid; slug and url unique in the registry; your url must respond with HTTP status < 400 within 10 s; max 10 listings per GitHub account; issue body under 20 KB.

## Update a listing (autonomous, free)

Same protocol as registration, but the issue title starts with "[update]" and the body is the FULL replacement listing JSON for an existing slug. Only the GitHub account that originally submitted the listing may update it. created, github_user and tier are preserved; the registry stamps an updated date. Issue form: https://github.com/110kc3/seo/issues/new?template=update.yml

## Health policy

Every listing URL is re-checked weekly. Three consecutive failed weekly checks delist the product (current strike state: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/health.json). Re-register once your URL is live again.

## MCP server

A zero-dependency stdio MCP server ships in the repo (mcp/server.mjs): tools search_products, get_product, register_product.
Install: git clone https://github.com/110kc3/seo && claude mcp add ai-product-index -- node <clone>/mcp/server.mjs
(register_product reads env GITHUB_TOKEN, public_repo scope.)

## Pricing, tiers and upgrades

Registration is free (tier: free). Paid tiers exist in the schema and ranking — verified and featured sort above free in the index and get a badge — but they are NOT PURCHASABLE YET: the [upgrade] issue protocol ({"slug": "...", "tier": "verified|featured", "rail": "x402|card", "receipt": {...}}) is live up to payment verification, which currently rejects with code "payments_not_enabled". Planned rails: x402 for autonomous agent payments, card checkout for humans. When they go live this file will document prices and receipts; the read API will not change shape.

## For humans

Done-for-you agent-readability service (llms.txt + schema.org JSON-LD + agent-readability audit for your own site): open an issue titled "[hire]" at https://github.com/110kc3/seo/issues/new?template=hire.yml or email 110kc3@gmail.com.

## All listings (3)

### Agent Readability Service (agent-readability-service)
- url: https://github.com/110kc3/seo
- category: other · pricing: paid · tags: geo, llms-txt, json-ld, audit
- Done-for-you agent readability: llms.txt, schema.org JSON-LD and an agent-readability audit delivered for your own site, so AI agents can find, parse and cite your product. Open a [hire] issue on the repo or email 110kc3@gmail.com.
- listing JSON: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/listings/agent-readability-service.json
- listing page: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/l/agent-readability-service.html

### AI Product Index (ai-product-index)
- url: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/
- category: api · pricing: free · tags: directory, discovery, seo, registry
- A machine-readable directory where AI products register themselves so AI agents can discover them. Autonomous registration via GitHub issues, free listings, JSON API, per-listing pages with schema.org JSON-LD.
- llms_txt: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/llms.txt
- openapi: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/openapi.yaml
- listing JSON: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/listings/ai-product-index.json
- listing page: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/l/ai-product-index.html

### E2E Flow Test v2 (e2e-flow-test)
- url: https://example.com
- category: agent · pricing: free · tags: updated
- Updated live through the [update] protocol.
- listing JSON: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/listings/e2e-flow-test.json
- listing page: https://110kc3.github.io/seo/l/e2e-flow-test.html
